RULING:
PLAINTIFFS' MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

No other appearance and tentative ruling adopted as amended: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). The trial date is March 5, 2018 at 11:15am in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is March 14, 2018. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is March 1, 2018. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than January 19, 2018. Responses to the demand are due no later than February 2, 2018. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands stating their best and final offers. These confidential demands must be submitted to Pang Ly by February 26, 2018 at 2:00p.m. Settlement conference in room 603 at 9:30a.m. on March 1, 2018. Fact discovery cut-off date is February 16, 2018. Expert discovery cut-off date is March 2, 2018. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than December 15, 2017. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents. (Mini Minutes Continued, see next entry.)
